message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="steve-schoger" color="cyan" summary="Sidebar dropped, progress bar dropped, aligned on all points">
Marty, thanks for the detailed response. A few important updates and clarifications:

## Sidebar Update -- We've Moved On

You agreed with the sidebar, but since that message, Heydon raised a strong accessibility concern and I've **dropped the sidebar in favor of a wrapping header nav**. The full team discussion happened between Lea, Heydon, and me. Here's the summary:

- **Problem**: A persistent sidebar is a visual spatial metaphor that doesn't translate to screen readers. It creates an extra landmark users navigate past on every page.
- **Decision**: All GTD view links live in the existing `<nav>` in the header. One landmark, linear document flow, flexbox wrap on mobile.
- **Result**: Simpler CSS, better accessibility, no sidebar tokens needed. Content area stays at ~44rem.

This is settled and Lea and I are aligned on the implementation. Your product requirements are fully met -- all 6 nav destinations are still one click away from any page.

## Inbox Amber Accent -- Agreed, Dropped

You're right to push back on the amber background for inbox items. I was overdesigning the urgency signal. The count badge in the nav is sufficient. Inbox items in the list will use the same clean treatment as other items -- no color coding. "Organized and under control" is exactly the right vibe.

## Progress Indicator Pushback -- Fully Agree

Your pushback on "3/7 items complete" is correct and Scott made the same point from the domain side. GTD projects are open-ended -- actions get added, removed, delegated. A progress bar implies a fixed total, which is misleading.

**Revised project card design:**
- Project title (`--font-size-lg`, `--font-semibold`)
- "X active next actions" as text count (`--font-size-sm`, `--color-fg-muted`)
- Stalled state: "No next action defined" in `--color-danger` text + `--color-warning` left border + inline "Add next action" link
- No progress bar, no fraction, no percentage

This is simpler and more honest about how GTD projects work.

## Completed Actions -- Aligned

Your recommendation matches mine: remove from Next Actions list, visible in project detail view. For unlinked completions (actions not tied to a project), I proposed a collapsed `<details>` element at the bottom of Next Actions: "Show completed (12)". This gives users a way to verify without cluttering the working view. Does that work for you?

## Context Tags -- Single Color Confirmed

Your instinct matches mine and Lea's: single muted accent color (teal), not a rainbow. Contexts are functional labels, not visual categories. The teal we chose (`oklch(0.40 0.08 200)` text on `oklch(0.92 0.03 200)` background) is distinct from our primary blue and passes AA contrast easily.

## Default Contexts -- One Change

Your list of 6 is close. I'd recommend dropping `@office` to get to 5: `@computer`, `@home`, `@errands`, `@phone`, `@anywhere`. Not everyone works in an office (remote workers, students, freelancers). Five fits the context filter bar without wrapping on most screens. Users who need `@office` can add it as a custom context.

## Summary of Where We Stand

All your product decisions are sound. The only requested change to the documents is the default contexts list (5 instead of 6). Everything else in the discovery doc, user stories, glossary, and future ideas is approved from a design perspective.

Ready for consensus whenever the full team has reviewed.
</teammate-message>

content.text:
Steve Schoger confirming design decisions that are already settled. All of these points have been discussed and aligned on in prior messages. No new information that affects Task #11.

The key items are already incorporated:
- Header nav (not sidebar) -- settled, Luca is implementing it
- No amber inbox accent -- settled
- No progress bars on projects -- settled
- Completed actions pattern with `<details>` -- noted for future
- Context tags single color -- noted for future
- Default contexts: 5 (drop @office) -- noted for step 3

Standing by for Luca's progress on the remaining 3 Task #11 items.
